name: Earthjustice
mission: Stay informed on how we hold accountable those who break our environmental laws.
theoryOfChange: Earthjustice has a published "Commitment To Justice" page that details a "justice framework" developed by their Community, Justice, and Impact (CJI) team. This framework outlines a clear vision for advancing justice in their interactions, choices, and work, aiming to achieve a truly democratic multi-racial society that incorporates and serves all people and the planet. It emphasizes critical inquiry as a key tool to interrogate choices and understand the organization's impact. The framework guides every staff member to engage in a practice of justice that is informed, builds interconnectedness, encourages inclusive innovation, and maintains values-based accountability.

scoreSummary: Earthjustice shows strong impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Annually recognized by the nation’s chief watchdog groups for charitable organizations for transparency and efficiency

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.evidence: Niche cause: UNKNOWN (+4); Serves underserved populations (+3)
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 27/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: Evaluation source domain 'charitywatch.org' is not a known trusted evaluator

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: High domestic burn: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

risks.description: High domestic burn: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

score_details.score_summary: Earthjustice shows strong impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://earthjusticehelp.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13119586070029-Can-I-make-a-donation-to-Earthjustice-through-my-Donor-Advised-Fund)

all_citations.claim: Earthjustice maintains a program expense ratio of 80.2% and operates programs such as The Wild and Healthy Communities.
The organization reported Total Revenue of $125,960,142 and holds 19.3 months of working capital.
Earthjustice provides various donation options for supporters to contribute general charitable giving or sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice earns a 61/100 score, reflecting exceptional financial health and fundraising efficiency balanced against the high-cost, long-term nature of legal advocacy. While its impact is significant through systemic change, the direct cost per beneficiary is higher than traditional service-based charities.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary due to the nature of complex legal advocacy
Lower alignment for donors specifically seeking direct humanitarian relief

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is moderate as the organization focuses on advocacy and systemic environmental change rather than direct humanitarian aid [1]. For those interested in sadaqah, the organization offers various general giving methods to support its mission of protecting healthy communities [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high capacity-building impact by utilizing its $125,960,142 in revenue to secure legal precedents [2]. While the cost per beneficiary is calculated at 118249.374 due to the advocacy-focused model, the 80.2% program expense Ratio ensures the vast majority of funds support direct legal action [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Earthjustice is a premier nonprofit public interest law organization dedicated to wielding the power of law to protect the environment and public health.
strengths: High operational efficiency with a program expense ratio of 80.2%
Strong financial stability with 19.3 months of working capital
Highly efficient fundraising at $0.12 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: Founded as a legal advocate for the natural world, Earthjustice operates major programs including The Wild and Healthy Communities to defend environmental protections [1]. The organization reported a Total Revenue of $125,960,142 and maintains a robust financial position to support its long-term litigation efforts [2].

all_citations.claim: protect healthy wild lands, wildlife, and oceans
all_citations.quote: Earthjustice fights to preserve healthy wild lands, wildlife, and oceans.

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice earns a 61/100 score, reflecting exceptional financial health and fundraising efficiency balanced against the high-cost, long-term nature of legal advocacy. While its impact is significant through systemic change, the direct cost per beneficiary is higher than traditional service-based charities.

areas_for_improvement.context: The nature of complex legal advocacy results in a high <cite id="13">cost of $118,249 per beneficiary</cite> compared to direct-aid charities.
Despite some international work, <cite id="14">80.2% of expenses stay in the US</cite>, limiting global reach in high-need areas.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization mitigates this by partnering with local grassroots groups through its grantmaking program.
risk_factors: <cite id="26">High domestic burn rate</cite> of 99% limits international impact.
case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the advocacy-heavy model: <cite id="25">legal battles can take years</cite> to show tangible results for communities.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and governance data available.

alignment.explanation: This charity is <cite id="22">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite> and focuses on advocacy, which may not align with donors seeking direct humanitarian relief. However, it serves <cite id="23">underserved populations</cite> and addresses climate urgency.
alignment.improvement: The charity could improve alignment for Muslim donors by clearly stating its <cite id="24">sadaqah-only status</cite> on its donation pages.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="16">100% independent board</cite> and long-standing track record since 1971. They provide <cite id="17">audited financial statements</cite> and maintain high transparency through Candid.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility further, the charity could document specific triggers for the use of its <cite id="18">high reserves</cite> to ensure funds are deployed effectively.

impact.explanation: Operations show a <cite id="19">80.2% program expense ratio</cite> with a focus on capacity building and systemic legal change. They have a <cite id="20">documented theory of change</cite> that guides their litigation strategy.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from more granular reporting on <cite id="21">outcomes beyond litigation wins</cite>, such as long-term health metrics in served communities.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Documented theory of change and verified outcomes

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports partners in environmental justice and conservation.

rich_narrative.headline: Earthjustice is a premier nonprofit public interest law organization dedicated to wielding the power of law to protect the environment and public health.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize systemic legal change and want to fund long-term environmental protections through the court system.
donor_motivations: Want to address the root causes of environmental degradation
Value legal expertise as a tool for social justice
Prefer funding established institutions with deep expertise
Care about protecting wild lands and public health
giving_considerations: Gifts support legal advocacy rather than direct material aid
Impact is often measured in court victories and policy changes
The organization has very high financial reserves
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate, direct humanitarian relief like food or medicine.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects a strong theory of change and verified outcomes, though the nature of advocacy makes RCT-level evidence difficult.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Earthjustice uses litigation to enforce environmental laws, creating precedents that protect ecosystems and public health.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in legal advocacy ensures that litigation strategies are actually resulting in improved environmental standards.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While they have high reserves, the scale of environmental litigation needs continues to grow.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="27">Tribal Sovereignty Support</cite> and massive working capital reserves.

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates high fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="7">$0.12 per $1 raised</cite> in fundraising efforts.
Earthjustice maintains <cite id="8">19.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer for long-term litigation.
The charity holds a <cite id="9">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, reflecting a commitment to public disclosure.

strengths_deep_dive: Earthjustice's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and financial resilience. Having operated for over 50 years, it has built a <cite id="10">board of 33 members</cite> that provides extensive oversight. Their financial health is bolstered by a <cite id="11">3-Year Revenue CAGR of 4.7%</cite>, showing steady growth despite economic fluctuations. Furthermore, the organization is a significant grantmaker, distributing <cite id="12">$8,145,100 in total grants</cite> to support local partners and grassroots environmental efforts.
rich_narrative.summary: Earthjustice is a premier nonprofit public interest law organization that has been <cite id="1">founded in 1971</cite> to defend the natural world and public health. The organization operates through a unique model of legal advocacy, wielding the power of law to <cite id="2">protect healthy wild lands, wildlife, and oceans</cite>. Unlike traditional charities that provide direct services, Earthjustice focuses on high-impact litigation to secure systemic environmental protections. In the 2023 tax year, the organization reported <cite id="3">Total Revenue of $125,960,142</cite>, demonstrating a significant scale of operations. Their work is organized into five core program areas, including <cite id="4">Climate and Energy, Healthy Communities, and Tribal Sovereignty Support</cite>. By representing clients free of charge, they ensure that the most vulnerable communities have a voice in the legal system. The charity maintains a strong financial foundation with <cite id="5">$238,324,316 in net assets</cite> as of 2023, which is critical for sustaining multi-year legal battles against powerful interests. Their efficiency is notable, as they maintain a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 80.2%</cite>, ensuring the vast majority of funds support their legal and advocacy mission. This focus on systemic change through the courts makes them a distinct player in the environmental sector.
